title: Allophylus rubifolius
taxonomy: Allophylus rubifolius is classified within the family Sapindaceae, which comprises about 130 genera of flowering plants. The genus Allophylus itself is native to tropical Africa and Asia, and contains around 400 species. Allophylus rubifolius belongs to the order Sapindales, which is an order of flowering plants that also includes other families like Anacardiaceae and Meliaceae. This classification helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships among plant species. The exact classification may be subject to change as new research and data become available.

intro: Allophylus rubifolius, also known as the red-leaf allomphus, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Sapindaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Allophylus rubifolius include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Allophylus rubifolius is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are consumed to alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
